items.q: What is the first step of formal analysis?
items.correct: Description - noting what is actually present in the work.
items.why: Students jump to meaning immediately. Formal analysis follows a sequence: describe, analyse formal elements, interpret, evaluate. Description grounds interpretation in what's actually there.

items.correct: An object's design should be determined by its purpose.
items.misconception: Decoration is the most important aspect of design.
items.random_wrong: Designers must follow strict government forms.
items.why: Students invert the meaning. Sullivan's modernist principle prioritises FUNCTION over ornament - the chair's shape should serve sitting, not the other way around. Postmodernism later challenged this.

items.q: What is appropriation in postmodern art?
items.correct: Deliberately reusing existing images or styles, often to comment on originality.
items.why: Students moralise about copying. Postmodern appropriation (Sherrie Levine, Richard Prince) is a CRITICAL STRATEGY - it questions authorship and originality, central modernist values.

items.q: Which technique uses crossing lines for tone?
items.why: It's Cross-hatching. “Stippling” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Dots. “Lines” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many primary colours are commonly used in pigment mixing?
items.why: Exact answer: 3 colours. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
